description_long: This kiosk is designed to resemble a miniature classical temple, featuring four robust columns supporting a triangular pediment adorned with ornate reliefs. The structure is elevated on a stone platform, accessed by a short staircase, though one of the steps appears broken and displaced. The base of the kiosk is decorated with intricate patterns and golden griffin motifs, adding a touch of mythological grandeur. The interior is open and roofless, with a partially collapsed roof slab resting on the steps, suggesting a state of partial ruin. The stone surfaces are weathered, with visible cracks and discoloration, evoking an ancient, historical ambiance. The overall appearance is both decorative and monumental, making it a striking feature in any urban setting.
description: A small, temple-like kiosk with four columns, ornate pediment, decorative griffins, and a partially ruined stone staircase, evoking an ancient classical style.
description_view_0: Front view shows the ornate pediment, two main columns, decorated base with griffins, and a broken staircase leading up to the open interior.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the structure, the thickness of the columns, and the collapsed roof slab resting on the steps.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the open back, the continuation of the decorative base, and the absence of a rear wall or door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the other side, showing the columns, decorative motifs, and the open, roofless interior.
attribute_roof_design: gabled roof with triangular pediment, partially collapsed, stone material, ornate reliefs
attribute_architectural_style: classical Greco-Roman with mythological decorative elements
